content.problemStatement:
You‚Äôre given an array AAA of size NNN.

Count the number of quadruples (L1,R1,L2,R2)(L_1,R_1,L_2,R_2)(L1‚Äč,R1‚Äč,L2‚Äč,R2‚Äč) satisfying:


1‚Č§L1‚Č§R1<L2‚Č§R2‚Č§N1 \le L_1 \le R_1 \lt L_2 \le R_2 \le N1‚Č§L1‚Äč‚Č§R1‚Äč<L2‚Äč‚Č§R2‚Äč‚Č§N;
gcd‚Ā°(AL1,‚Ä¶,AR1)√ógcd‚Ā°(AL2,‚Ä¶,AR2)\gcd(A_{L_1},\ldots ,A_{R_1}) \times \gcd(A_{L_2},\ldots,A_{R_2})gcd(AL1‚Äč‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AR1‚Äč‚Äč)√ógcd(AL2‚Äč‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AR2‚Äč‚Äč) is a perfect square. Note that gcd‚Ā°\gcdgcd denoted the greatest common divisor.
Since the answer may be huge, output the answer modulo 998244353998244353998244353.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN.
The second lines contains NNN integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1,A_2,\ldots,A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč.




Output Format
For each test case, output in a new line, the number of quadruples, modulo 998244353998244353998244353.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 4‚čÖ1054 \cdot 10^54‚čÖ105.
